name: semantic_issues_search
description:
Search for issues using natural language queries within a GitHub repository or across the user's top repositories.
					This tool uses embedded vector data to find relevant issues,
					even if they don't contain your exact keywords.
					The embeddings are pre-computed and updated regularly,
					so the search is a fast and cheap way to find related issues.

					Use this tool when:
					- You want to find issues related to a concept or topic
					- You want related / similar issues without enumerating every keyword
					- You're exploring or de-duplicating problem reports
					- You are researching introspection queries about a repo (such as when asking about the most requested features or progress on a specific feature), since Issues represent the planning & tracking portion of the work

					Benefits:
					- Captures synonyms & paraphrases (e.g. "screen reader focus loss" vs "VoiceOver loses focus")
					- Reduces missed matches from narrow keyword lists

					Examples:
					- Find authentication bugs: query="authentication login errors", owner="octocat", repo="Hello-World"
					- Find open issues about auth authored by a specific user: query="state:open author:monalisa User permissions, access control, and authentication errors", owner="octocat", repo="Hello-World"
					- Find performance problems: query="slow rendering performance", owner="octocat", repo="Hello-World"
					- Find issues across the user's top repositories: query="memory leak"

					If no repository is specified, searches across the user's top repositories. For more targeted results, specify a specific repository.

owner.description: Repository owner. Required if `repo` is specified.

query.description: Natural language query with optional GitHub search qualifiers. Supports semantic matching. Examples: 'authentication login errors', 'state:open author:username performance issues'. Supports advanced GitHub issue search syntax for filtering by state, author, labels, etc.

repo.description: Repository name. Required if `owner` is specified.

sort.description: Sort field for search results. Defaults to best match.
